source_metadata.title_raw: Adam Wachter - A-18-041 - June 1, 2018 - Redondo Beach

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacrnmento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Adam Wachter 
1503 Harkness Lane 
Redondo Beach, CA 90278 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-041 
Dear Mr. Wachter: 
Junel,2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding campaign disclosure provisions of 
the Political Reform Act ("Act").1 
Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby ( 1975) I 
FPPC Ops. 71 ); any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not 
the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
Do you need to reopen your campaign bank account to satisfy a judgment stemming from 
services provided during your campaign for the Redondo Beach City Council, if you pay the 
judgment using personal funds? 
CONCLUSION 
No. It is not necessary to reopen the campaign bank account. However, because payment of 
the judgment would be directly related to your campaign, you must report the activity by filing an 
additional campaign statement (Form 460) listing your personal contribution coming in on Schedule 
A and the funds going out to the vendor on Schedule E as an expenditure. 
FACTS 
You were a candidate for Redondo Beach City Council last year and lost your campaign and 
have since closed your bank account. Following your campaign, you became aware that a lawsuit 
was filed against you by a consultant for services rendered during the campaign. The suit concluded 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indica1ed. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are lo Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-041 
Page No. 2 
with a $3,000 judgment entered against you. You intend on paying the judgment using your 
personal funds. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act requires candidates and committees to file periodic campaign statements disclosing 
contributions received and expenditures made in connection with state and local elections (Section 
84100, et seq.) A "campaign statement" is defined as "an itemized report which is prepared on a 
form prescribed by the Commission .... " (Section 82006.) Campaign statements are filed on paper 
at specified times and places depending on the location and activities of the particular candidate or 
committee. (Sections 84200-84215.) 
You advised that a consultant brought a personal claim in a court of law against you 
regarding services provided during your campaign for a position on the Redondo Beach City 
Council. A $3,000 judgment was entered against you. Generally, the Act requires candidates to 
establish a campaign bank account into which all campaign contributions must be deposited and 
from which all campaign expenditures must be made. (Section 85201.) Under your specific facts, 
requiring you to open a separate bank account for one transaction using your personal funds would 
impose burdensome requirements without serving a legitimate public purpose. 
Nonetheless, because payment of this judgment would be directly related to your campaign, 
you must report the transaction by filing an additional campaign statement (Form 460) disclosing 
receipt of your personal funds on Schedule A and the payment to the consultant on Schedule E. You 
do not need to reopen your campaign bank account so long as you use your personal funds to satisfy 
the judgment in one single payment and do not raise funds from others. You must also maintain 
records of the judgment and your payment of the judgment pursuant to the recordkeeping 
requirements in Regulation 18401. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
RPOC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
By: 
Ryan P. O'Connor 
Counsel, Legal Division
